title: Tapinanthus engleri
taxonomy: Tapinanthus engleri is a species of flowering plant in the family Loranthaceae, commonly known as mistletoes. It is a type of hemiparasitic plant that grows on the branches of other trees, obtaining some of its nutrients from the host plant. The Loranthaceae family is a group of flowering plants that are native to tropical and subtropical regions around the world. These plants are known for their unique ability to parasitize their host plants, often causing little to no harm to the host tree. Tapinanthus engleri is native to tropical Africa, specifically in the countries of Democratic Republic of the Congo, Tanzania, and Zambia.
